skill.name: setup-dev-env
skill.description: Guides an agent through project development environment setup, including dependency checks, builds, database startup, migrations, and environment file verification.
skill.summary: Set up backend, frontend, and database dependencies for a local development project.
skill.author: Crearize

security_audit.summary: Static external command findings are true positives because the skill instructs agents to run Java, Node, pnpm, Docker Compose, Gradle, and migration commands. The environment file findings are partially true: the skill checks for backend/.env and frontend/.env.local paths, but it does not read or exfiltrate their contents. No evidence found of malicious intent, network exfiltration, or prompt injection; the weak cryptography finding at line 3 is a false positive.

medium_findings.title: Repository-Controlled Setup Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run dependency installation, build, Docker Compose, and database migration commands. This is expected for a development setup skill, but these commands can execute repository-controlled build scripts or install hooks and should require user approval.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The shell commands are explicit and numerous, so the external command risk is real. The confidence is not higher because the commands match the stated development setup purpose and show no exfiltration behavior.

medium_findings.title: Environment File Path Checks
medium_findings.description: The skill checks for backend/.env and frontend/.env.local and includes those paths in the completion report. It does not display file contents, but environment files often contain secrets and should not be read or summarized.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment file references are direct evidence. Risk is moderate because only existence checks are shown, with no evidence that secret values are accessed.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Is Unsupported
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptography at line 3, but that line is the skill description. No evidence found of a cryptographic algorithm, hash function, or encryption operation in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 contains descriptive text about development environment setup, not crypto logic. This is a strong false positive based on the inspected file content.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Blocks
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill contains command blocks that would run local tools, install dependencies, start containers, build code, and execute migrations. These commands are not malicious by themselves, but they can trigger untrusted project code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell command blocks are directly visible in the skill file. The pattern is dangerous in context because install and build commands may run repository-defined scripts.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hidden Environment File References
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill references hidden environment files in backend and frontend folders. The shown commands only check for file presence, but secret-bearing files require careful handling.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The hidden environment file paths are explicit. The risk is bounded because there is no instruction to print or transmit file contents.

content.value_statement: Local project setup often fails because backend, frontend, database, and environment checks happen in different places. This skill gives Claude, Codex, or Claude Code a clear sequence for setup and verification.
actual_capabilities: Checks Java, Node.js, pnpm, Docker, and Docker Compose availability.
Prepares a backend project by enabling the Gradle wrapper and running a build without tests.
Installs frontend dependencies with pnpm in the frontend directory.
Starts a PostgreSQL database service through Docker Compose.
Runs Flyway migrations through the backend Gradle wrapper.
Verifies backend and frontend build commands after setup.
limitations: It assumes a backend directory with a Gradle wrapper.
It assumes a frontend directory that uses pnpm.
It assumes Docker Compose has a database service named db.
It checks environment file presence but does not create or validate secret values.

use_cases.title: Onboard a New Developer
use_cases.description: Run a repeatable setup checklist for a project that has backend, frontend, and database components.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Fresh Local Workspace
use_cases.description: Verify required tools, install dependencies, start the database, and confirm builds before feature work begins.

use_cases.title: Validate Project Setup Documentation
use_cases.description: Use the skill flow to compare documented setup steps against the commands needed by the project.

prompt_templates.title: Check My Tools
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the setup-dev-env skill to check whether this workspace has the required Java, Node.js, pnpm, Docker, and Docker Compose tools. Report missing tools only.

prompt_templates.title: Set Up Frontend Only
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the setup-dev-env skill for the frontend target only. Install dependencies, run the frontend build, and summarize any errors without changing unrelated files.

prompt_templates.title: Set Up Backend and Database
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the setup-dev-env skill for backend and database setup. Ask before running Docker or migration commands, then report build and migration results.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Full Workspace Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the setup-dev-env skill for the full project. Before executing commands, list the planned commands and explain which ones may run project-defined scripts.

output_examples.input: Check whether my local tools are ready.
output_examples.output: The setup review would report detected Java, Node.js, pnpm, Docker, and Docker Compose versions, then list any missing prerequisites.

output_examples.input: Prepare the backend and database for local development.
output_examples.output: The result would summarize Gradle build status, database container startup, migration status, and any setup step that failed.

output_examples.input: Run the full setup and tell me what to do next.
output_examples.output: The completion report would cover backend, frontend, database, environment file presence, and the next commands for starting development servers.

best_practices: Review each command before execution, especially install, build, Docker, and migration steps.
Run the skill in the intended project root so backend and frontend paths resolve correctly.
Confirm environment files exist without printing secret values into chat or logs.
anti_patterns: Do not run setup commands in an untrusted repository without reviewing build scripts first.
Do not paste environment variable secrets into the assistant conversation.
Do not assume the database service name is correct without checking the project Docker Compose file.

faq.question: Does this skill install system tools?
faq.answer: No. It checks tool availability and runs project setup commands, but it does not install Java, Node.js, pnpm, Docker, or Docker Compose.

faq.question: Can it create missing environment files?
faq.answer: No. It checks for backend and frontend environment files, but it does not generate secret values or write new environment files.

faq.question: Does it support projects without Gradle?
faq.answer: Not directly. The backend flow assumes a Gradle wrapper exists in the backend directory.

faq.question: Does it support package managers other than pnpm?
faq.answer: Not directly. The frontend flow is written for pnpm and would need changes for npm, Yarn, or Bun.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill runs setup commands that may execute project-defined scripts. This is useful, but it requires user review in community repositories.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
